description:
Step 3 of T-012's 4-step CSP roadmap. CLOSED as decision-only (no refactor) — see commit on this ticket and `app/middleware/csp_nonce.py` module docstring for the durable record.

## Decision (2026-05-19)

**Keep `'unsafe-inline'` in CSP `style-src` permanently.**

Rationale:
- Vigilant is a single-tenant authenticated EVE companion app with no untrusted user-generated HTML surface. Style-based XSS is dramatically less exploitable than script injection in this threat model.
- Refactoring 3,296 inline `style="..."` declarations across 101 templates to JS-driven `element.style.setProperty(...)` calls is a months-long undertaking with real first-paint regressions and high visual-regression risk.
- Industry precedent (GitHub, Linear, Notion, Stripe Dashboard) keeps `style-src 'unsafe-inline'` while tightening `script-src`. OWASP CSP cheat-sheet and Google's web.dev "Strict CSP" guide both treat this stance as acceptable.
- The marginal hardening from dropping `style-src 'unsafe-inline'` is not worth the cost.

## What landed

- `app/middleware/csp_nonce.py`: module docstring rewritten to document the decision and the new 4-step roadmap status (Step 2 ongoing under T-031, Step 3 closed by this ticket, Step 4 scope-reduced for T-033). Inline comment block added directly above `_CSP_TEMPLATE` to flag the decision at the template site so future contributors don't accidentally "complete" the work.
- No template changes. The 3,296 inline styles stay.

## Implications

- **T-033 scope shrinks**: now only flips `script-src` to enforcing; `style-src` keeps `'unsafe-inline'`.
- **ISS-028** filed for design-system hygiene consolidation of the top-5 repeated inline-style patterns (~250 occurrences, mechanically sed-replaceable). Independent of CSP; pure DX.

## Done

Ticket closes with the decision documented durably. Future contributors who want to revisit must open a new ticket and re-evaluate the threat model.
title: CSP Step 3: refactor 101 templates with inline style= attrs to utility classes (VVP-2026-007)
